metadata.name: dwm-status-bar
metadata.summary: Add status information to your Dynamic Window Manager bar

classes.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.datetime_module.DateTimeModule
documentation:
A class representing a date and time module for system information.
It generates the current date and/or time according to the format
string provided by the user as the 'format_str' keyword argument.
The resulting string can be obtained by accessing the 'info'
readonly property.

It accepts the following optional parameters as keyword arguments:

Options:
    format_str: str
        Sets the format string to be used for the date and time.
        [default:  "%A %d/%m/%Y %R"]

    label: str
        A label to identify the information on each module.
        [default: None]

    delay: float
        Sets the time it takes to update the module's information,
        in seconds. [default: 1.0]

classes.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.disk_module.DiskModule
documentation:
A class representing a disk module for system information retrieval.
It generates the total disk space used for a particular partition.
The value is available as string with the percentage number through
the 'info' readonly property.

It accepts the following optional parameters as keyword arguments:

Options:
    path: str
        Sets the mountpoint path for a partition. [default: "/"]

    width: int
        Sets the total size of the resulting string. [default: 4]

    precision: int
        Sets the desired number of decimals in the resulting number.
        If the number gets too large, the precision is reduced in
        order to fit within the assigned 'width' parameter.
        [default: 1]

    label: str
        A label to identify the information on each module.
        [default: None]

    delay: float
        Sets the time it takes to update the module's information,
        in seconds. [default: 1.0]

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.battery_module.NetBSDBatteryModule.info
documentation:
Returns battery information as a formatted string.
If no battery is installed, returns None.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.battery_module.DefaultBatteryModule.info
documentation:
Returns battery information as a formatted string.
If no battery is installed, returns None.

classes.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.battery_module.BatteryModule
documentation:
A class representing a battery module for system information
retrieval. It generates the current energy available in percent
numbers as a string through the 'info' readonly property. If no
battery is present, the 'info' property will return None.

It accepts the following optional parameters as keyword arguments:

Options:
    width: int
        Sets the total size of the resulting string. [default: 5]

    precision: int
        Sets the desired number of decimals in the resulting number.
        If the number gets too large, the precision is reduced in
        order to fit within the assigned 'width' parameter.
        [default: 2]

    label: str
        A label to identify the information on each module.
        [default: None]

    delay: float
        Sets the time it takes to update the module's information,
        in seconds. [default: 1.0]

classes.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.base_module.BaseModule

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.base_module.BaseModule.info
documentation: The module's information as a formatted string.

This is a base class for dwm bar modules. Each module should return
its relevant information as a formatted string through a 'info'
property. The information from all the modules will be condensed and
then printed in the bar.

classes.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.__new__
documentation:
The application entrypoint. Creates a new instance of the
status bar application, calls the initialization method and
returns the status code to the calling process upon exiting.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.init
documentation: Initializes the application before starting the main loop.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.main
documentation:
Instantiate the bar and execute it as an async coroutine.

After all the tasks finish execution, the application either
exits, or re-reads its configuration on SIGHUP.

If any error occurs, it gets printed to stderr and to a log
file.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.get_configs
documentation: Read and parse config information from an XML file.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.parse_args
documentation: Read and parse commandline arguments.

inner_classes.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.BarLogger

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.BarLogger.__init__
documentation: Set attributes and asynchronous signal handlers.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.BarLogger.__exit__
documentation: Ensure graceful exit upon cancelling tasks.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.BarLogger.__call__
documentation: Start running tasks.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.BarLogger.close
documentation: Cancel every running task.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.BarLogger._log_info
documentation: Retrieve information and write to the bar.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.app.App.BarLogger._create_watcher
documentation: Create a new task for a given module.

This class sends information to the status bar. It receives a
Config object and use it to write and update information on the
bar.

It is meant to be used as a context manager, which returns an
async callable object that starts the loop. Ex:

    with BarLogger(configs) as bar:
        await bar()
The entrypoint class that starts the application.

classes.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.cpu_module.CPUModule
documentation:
A class representing a CPU module for system information retrieval.
It generates the current CPU usage in percent numbers as a string,
available through the 'info' readonly property.

It accepts the following optional parameters as keyword arguments:

Options:
    interval: float
        Sets the interval time for comparing system CPU times.
        If interval is 0.0 or None, then the class returns the CPU
        times elapsed since the last call.
        If interval > 0.0, then the class's 'info' getter will block
        execution upon value retrieval.
        [default: None]

    width: int
        Sets the total size of the resulting string. [default: 4]

    precision: int
        Sets the desired number of decimals in the resulting number.
        If the number gets too large, the precision is reduced in
        order to fit within the assigned 'width' parameter.
        [default: 1]

    label: str
        A label to identify the information on each module.
        [default: None]

    delay: float
        Sets the time it takes to update the module's information,
        in seconds. [default: 1.0]

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.ram_module.LinuxRAMModule._read_meminfo
documentation: Return memory usage information as a dictionary.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.ram_module.NetBSDRAMModule._read_meminfo
documentation: Return memory usage information as a dictionary.

classes.name: dwm_status_bar.modules.ram_module.RAMModule
documentation:
A class representing a RAM module for system information retrieval.
It generates the current RAM usage in percent numbers as a string,
available through the 'info' readonly property.

It accepts the following optional parameters as keyword arguments:

Options:
    width: int
        Sets the total size of the resulting string. [default: 4]

    precision: int
        Sets the desired number of decimals in the resulting number.
        If the number gets too large, the precision is reduced in
        order to fit within the assigned 'width' parameter.
        [default: 1]

    label: str
        A label to identify the information on each module.
        [default: None]

    delay: float
        Sets the time it takes to update the module's information,
        in seconds. [default: 1.0]

functions.name: dwm_status_bar.util.trait
documentation:
A decorator to be used with class definitions. Dynamically add any
functions passed as arguments to a class as methods. You can also
choose method names by passing them as keyword arguments.

functions.name: dwm_status_bar.util.number_size
documentation:
Return how many characters are necessary to write a number. This
function should only be used for numbers ranging from 0 to 100.

functions.name: dwm_status_bar.util.format_number
documentation:
Format a number with a given width and precision. If the resulting
string representation is too large, the precision is reduced so the
number can fit within the chosen width.

functions.name: dwm_status_bar.util.find_process
documentation:
Find the previous running instance of the current script.
If more than one process is found, raise an error.

functions.name: dwm_status_bar.util.close
documentation: Close the bar process.

modules.name: dwm_status_bar.methods
documentation:
This script defines default methods for use with the module classes.
Each method described here can be used with the 'trait' decorator to
apply it directly to a class.

functions.name: dwm_status_bar.methods.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the module with optional parameters. Each optional
parameter passed this way must be provided as a keyword argument.

functions.name: dwm_status_bar.methods.__repr__
documentation: Showcase a bar module object with its attributes.

functions.name: dwm_status_bar.methods.__new__
documentation:
Create a proper instance of the module according to
the host machine's operating system.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.config.Config._holds_text
documentation:
Returns True if node is an element with text and no child elements
and False otherwise.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.config.Config._purge_whitespace
documentation: Removes unwanted whitespace from XML nodes.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.config.Config._read_config_file
documentation: Parse XML configuration file.

methods.name: dwm_status_bar.config.Config._is_module
documentation: Return True if cls is a subclass of BaseModule.

An object that holds configuration parameters for the bar modules.
It reads the configuration file and parses it into a proper format
to be read by the App object.
